name: Koppers Holdings Inc.
business: We are a leading integrated global provider of treated wood products, wood preservation chemicals and carbon compounds. Our products and services are used in a variety of niche applications in a diverse range of end-markets, including the railroad, specialty chemical, utility, residential lumber, agriculture, aluminum, steel, rubber and construction industries. We serve our customers through a comprehensive global manufacturing and distribution network.

exposures.sentence: Utility products include the pressure treatment of transmission and distribution poles for electric and telephone utilities.
We are a leading integrated global provider of treated wood products, wood preservation chemicals and carbon compounds.
The principal consumers of our carbon pitch are primary aluminum smelters.
Market demand for utility poles is expected to grow over the next few years. The main driver for growth is the construction of datacenters that support artificial intelligence development.
Other drivers of pole demand include aging pole infrastructure, the expansion of renewable energy, vehicle electrification, grid-hardening measures, and extreme weather protection.
Other drivers of pole demand include aging pole infrastructure, the expansion of renewable energy, vehicle electrification, grid-hardening measures, and extreme weather protection.

The principal use of our wood preservation chemicals is in the manufacture of treated lumber, which is used mainly for residential applications, such as wood decking, and also industrial applications, such as the treating of railroad crossties and utility poles.

catalysts.event: Idle Utility and Industrial Products facility in Vance, Alabama
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we made the decision to idle production activities at our Utility and Industrial Products facility in Vance, Alabama, effective immediately.

catalysts.event: Complete idling of Railroad Products and Services facility in Florence, South Carolina
catalysts.sentence: We expect to ramp down production at Florence over the next several months with plant idling activities to be completed by November 2026.

catalysts.event: Cessation of phthalic anhydride production at Stickney, Illinois
catalysts.sentence: Production of phthalic anhydride ceased in the second quarter of 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Installed base of approximately 450 million wood crossties in North America
tamClaims.sentence: The primary end-markets for RUPS are the North American railroad industry, which has an installed base of approximately 450 million wood crossties, and the U.S. and Australian utility industries which utilize wooden distribution and transmission poles.

tamClaims.claim: 2026 crosstie purchases estimated at 19.9 million ties, 13.3 million for Class I railroads
tamClaims.sentence: The Railway Tie Association's estimate of total crosstie purchases in 2026 is approximately 19.9 million ties, with approximately 13.3 million for Class I railroads.

tamClaims.claim: Historical annual crosstie demand range of 18 million to 22 million ties
tamClaims.sentence: Over the past few years, North American demand for crossties has been in the range of 18 million to 22 million crossties annually.

tamClaims.claim: LIRA projects year-over-year remodeling spending growth of 2.9% in early 2026, easing to 1.6% by end of 2026
tamClaims.sentence: The Leading Indicator of Remodeling Activity (LIRA) reported by the Joint Center for Housing Studies of Harvard University projects that year-over-year spending for annual homeowner renovation and maintenance expenditures is expected to grow by 2.9 percent in early 2026 before easing to 1.6 percent by the end of 2026.
